caption: Northern cardinal at the backyard feeder just after sunrise. He waits for the safflower seed and scolds the squirrels. #backyardbirding
Osprey working the water off the point at Quiet Waters Park this morning. Patience finally paid off. #quietwaterspark #chesapeakebay
First watercolor of the season — a great blue heron from a sketch I did along the Severn. Still learning to let the paper breathe. #watercolor
Evening light over the Severn River from the Naval Academy seawall on my morning walk route. Hard to beat this stretch of the Chesapeake. #annapolis
A pair of Carolina chickadees at the feeder. Small birds, big personalities. Thanks to everyone at @quietwaters_birders for the ID tips. #backyardbirding
Great egret wading the shallows at Quiet Waters. Stood still long enough that the camera almost forgot it was there. #quietwaterspark #birdwatching
Belted kingfisher rattling along the creek. Never quite quick enough to catch him dive, but this one held still a moment. #chesapeakebay #birding
Watercolor study of a goldfinch for the reading pact with my granddaughter — she picked the bird, I did the painting. #watercolor #backyardbirding
Northern cardinal at the feeder just after sunrise. He scolds the squirrels. #backyardbirding
A pair of goldfinches on the safflower this morning. Late for the season. #birding
Blue jay bullying everyone off the platform feeder again. #backyardbirds
Downy woodpecker on the suet, patient as ever. #birdwatching
First junco of the winter — cold's coming. #backyardbirding
Carolina wren singing from the oak before the rain. #birding
Red-bellied woodpecker, misnamed as always. #backyardbirds
Tufted titmouse, quick as a thought. #birdwatching
White-throated sparrow scratching in the leaf litter. #birding
A cooper's hawk cleared the feeder in one pass. Nature's audit. #backyardbirding
Chickadees working the sunflower hearts in the cold. #birdwatching
Mourning doves lined up on the wire like a quiet committee. #birding
House finch in worn winter red. #backyardbirds
Great blue heron on the Severn at dawn. #chesapeake
Osprey back on the nest platform — spring at last. #chesapeake
First graded wash that behaved itself. Small victories. #watercolor
Wet-on-wet sky study from the First Strokes lesson. #watercolorpractice
Tried the seawall in three washes. The water fought me. #watercolor
Cardinal sketch in watercolor — harder than the photo. #birdart
Loose floral study. Marion's hydrangeas from memory. #watercolor
Color-mixing chart. Cobalt and burnt sienna make a good gray. #watercolorbasics
Winter tree line, minimal palette. #watercolor
The Severn in ten minutes, plein air, cold hands. #pleinair
Fishing morning on the Severn with Jack. He out-caught me. #grandkids
Emma's play tonight. Third row, proud grandfather. #family
Gettysburg with Emma and her sketchbook. #history
Sunday cocoa with the grandkids. #family
Iron Rooster biscuits after the market. Worth the wait. #annapolis
